Test","version":1},"userdoc":{"kind":"user","methods":{"test_Boundary_BalanceDrain_AboveThreshold()":{"notice":"Balance drain at 501 bps (5.01%) SHOULD trigger"},"test_Boundary_BalanceDrain_AtThreshold()":{"notice":"Balance drain at exactly 500 bps (5.0%) SHOULD trigger"},"test_Boundary_BalanceDrain_BelowThreshold()":{"notice":"Balance drain at exactly 499 bps (4.99%) should NOT trigger"},"test_Boundary_GradualDrain_AtThreshold()":{"notice":"Gradual drain at 1500 bps (15.0%) SHOULD trigger         Uses 5 samples so each per-block drop stays under 5%."},"test_Boundary_GradualDrain_BelowThreshold()":{"notice":"Gradual drain at 1499 bps (14.99%) should NOT trigger         Uses 5 samples so each per-block drop stays well under the 5% single-block threshold."},"test_Boundary_NonceJump_AboveLimit()":{"notice":"Nonce jump of 6 SHOULD trigger (exceeds >5 threshold)"},"test_Boundary_NonceJump_AtLimit()":{"notice":"Nonce jump of exactly 5 should NOT trigger (threshold is >5)"},"test_DetectsBalanceDrain()":{"notice":"Test that balance drain is also detected (secondary signal)"},"test_DetectsGradualDrain()":{"notice":"Detect gradual drain across window"},"test_DetectsGuardRemoval()":{"notice":"Detect guard removal"},"test_DetectsImplementationSwap()":{"notice":"Core test: shouldRespond fires on implementation swap"},"test_DetectsModuleAddition()":{"notice":"Detect module addition"},"test_DetectsNonceJump()":{"notice":"Detect nonce jump indicating rapid transaction execution"},"test_DetectsOwnerSetChange()":{"notice":"Detect owner set change (different signers, same count)"},"test_DetectsSubtleMasterCopySwap()":{"notice":"Detect subtle masterCopy swap where new implementation still has Safe functions"},"test_EdgeCase_BothSamplesEmpty()":{"notice":"Both samples zero-length should return (false, \"\")"},"test_EdgeCase_EmptyDataArray()":{"notice":"Empty data array should return (false, \"\") -- not revert"},"test_EdgeCase_MalformedBytes()":{"notice":"Malformed bytes (too short to be a valid Snapshot) should return (false, \"\")"},"test_EdgeCase_SingleSample()":{"notice":"Single sample (no comparison possible) should return (false, \"\")"},"test_EdgeCase_ZeroLengthCurrentSample()":{"notice":"Zero-length bytes in data[0] should return (false, \"\") -- not revert"},"test_EdgeCase_ZeroLengthPreviousSample()":{"notice":"Zero-length bytes in data[1] should return (false, \"\") -- not revert"},"test_EndToEnd_DetectRespondContain()":{"notice":"End-to-end: Detect -> Respond -> Contain cycle using real exploit data.   This test demonstrates the FULL Drosera response path:   1. Trap collects data at the real exploit blocks   2. shouldRespond() fires with the correct threat type and payload   3. The payload is passed directly to the responder's handleIncident()   4. Responder pauses operations and logs the incident   5. While paused, further operations are blocked   6. Admin resolves the incident and unpauses   This proves payload alignment end-to-end:   trap shouldRespond() -> abi.encode(uint8, bytes) -> handleIncident(uint8, bytes)"},"test_ExploitReproduction_BybitAttack()":{"notice":"EXPLOIT REPRODUCTION: Replay the Bybit $1.46B hack step-by-step.   Step 1 (Block 21,895,237): Pre-exploit -- Safe proxy points to legitimate           singleton, all functions work, wallet holds ~$1.46B in ETH + LSTs.   Step 2 (Block 21,895,238): masterCopy swap -- Attacker's delegatecall           overwrites slot 0 with malicious implementation address.           Safe functions now revert (malicious contract doesn't implement them).           But ALL FUNDS ARE STILL IN THE WALLET -- 18-block response window.   Step 3 (Block 21,895,256): Drain -- Attacker calls backdoor functions on           the malicious implementation to sweep all funds.           401,346 ETH + 90,375 stETH + 8,000 mETH + 15,000 cmETH = $1.46B gone.   THIS IS WHERE DROSERA INTERVENES: Between Step 2 and Step 3.   The trap detects the masterCopy swap at Step 2 and triggers the response   contract to pause operations -- 18 blocks BEFORE the drain."},"test_FullOperatorFlow()":{"notice":"Full Drosera operator simulation with 5 block samples"},"test_NoFalsePositive()":{"notice":"No false positive during normal operation"},"test_PostSwapState()":{"notice":"Post-swap: Safe functions revert, ETH still present (18-block window)"},"test_PreExploitState()":{"notice":"Pre-exploit: Safe functions work, balances are large"},"test_ResponderIntegration()":{"notice":"Verify the responder correctly receives and stores incident data"},"test_SampleOrdering_IdenticalSnapshots()":{"notice":"Verify that identical consecutive snapshots do not trigger"},"test_SampleOrdering_SwappedOrderNoFalsePositive()":{"notice":"Verify data[0] = newest, data[1] = previous convention is respected.         Swapping order (putting pre-exploit in data[0]) should NOT trigger,         because a \"recovering\" balance is not a drain."}},"notice":"Validates that the BybitSafeTrap detects implementation swaps,         balance drains, module changes, guard changes, owner manipulation,         gradual drains, and nonce jumps \u2014 with correctly wired response payloads.         Timeline:           Block 21,895,237 -- pre-exploit (everything normal)           Block 21,895,238 -- masterCopy swapped via delegatecall (DETECTION POINT 1)           Block 21,895,256 -- ETH drained from wallet (DETECTION POINT 2)           => 18-block window for Drosera to trigger emergency response         Exploit TX: 0x46deef0f52e3a983b67abf4714448a41dd7ffd6d32d32da69d62081c68ad7882","version":1}},"settings":{"compilationTarget":{"test/v1/BybitSafeTrap.t.sol":"BybitSafeTrapTest"},"evmVersion":"prague","libraries":{},"metadata":{"bytecodeHash":"ipfs"},"optimizer":{"enabled":true,"runs":200},"remappings":[":drosera-contracts/=node_modules/drosera-contracts/src/",":forge-std/=node_modules/forge-std/src/"],"viaIR":true},"sources":{"node_modules/drosera-contracts/src/interfaces/ITrap.sol":{"keccak256":"0xd9b721aa99b335faa62d075696744e25a45e64a666f49bbf64f74b2382ca1b61","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://d1cdfbf39b4e05a0627d17998587adff461ebf7e74b22b72e0cb6839427a46f5","dweb:/ipfs/Qmc8inn3cSbrAyKtXWfwkcor8UVbVkufNBL8iKXY34edq6"]},"node_modules/forge-std/src/Base.sol":{"keccak256":"0x4ff1a785311017d1eedb1b4737956fa383067ad34eb439abfec1d989754dde1c","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://f553622969b9fdb930246704a4c10dfaee6b1a4468c142fa7eb9dc292a438224","dweb:/ipfs/QmcxqHnqdQsMVtgsfH9VNLmZ3g7GhgNagfq7yvNCDcCHFK"]},"node_modules/forge-std/src/StdAssertions.sol":{"keccak256":"0xf513292ab066e6017db48ca749c1e63a44dfc5cba0326fc65c718f96e029d361","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://02d715ae2898f1fabd08133f80e6a7a3b87a2796ca5d23d1cb3fed9f4367f34c","dweb:/ipfs/QmfESqa4j3PQAvvdfrnDQ1xUH1TnVCfvZYHsAEDK8z4X4k"]},"node_modules/forge-std/src/StdChains.sol":{"keccak256":"0x9cc29c28f49d0b7f7b2f1aa32d8273f8a087bf62eb3fb22d893df824052c25ef","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://42dcb36cb10b878a0d5b20ce3a4a3ba4f51f44d7731a66ac1133c699bc80b31b","dweb:/ipfs/QmY6q7SaHQMLBb3rS6xZdArPaXoskWeqF6oJwUeZ3gKLZj"]},"node_modules/forge-std/src/StdCheats.sol":{"keccak256":"0xb72f3519e0bf7d31df5d68557525f4fc55d861c3fb3b0f7793144ef7c94cbeb7","license":"MIT","urls":["bzz-raw://f3456d0f78e6f61203fa7871ba2df0d35925f10db3baee14be623ce2a35b84e2","dweb:/ipfs/QmWE6QQSBvJifHMraisBTrf1x4WCwrDoTPLX8UKajTiApc"]},"node_modules/forge-std/src/StdError.sol":{"keccak256":"0xbf477b11a42d7611696956546bcfaa29317d1166bf65e402344599c05943fc77","lic test_Boundary_BalanceDrain_AboveThreshol.notice: Balance drain at 501 bps (5.01%) SHOULD trigger

test_Boundary_BalanceDrain_AtThreshold().notice: Balance drain at exactly 500 bps (5.0%) SHOULD trigger

test_Boundary_BalanceDrain_BelowThreshol.notice: Balance drain at exactly 499 bps (4.99%) should NOT trigger

test_Boundary_GradualDrain_AtThreshold().notice: Gradual drain at 1500 bps (15.0%) SHOULD trigger         Uses 5 samples so each per-block drop stays under 5%.

test_Boundary_GradualDrain_BelowThreshol.notice: Gradual drain at 1499 bps (14.99%) should NOT trigger         Uses 5 samples so each per-block drop stays well under the 5% single-block threshold.

test_Boundary_NonceJump_AboveLimit().notice: Nonce jump of 6 SHOULD trigger (exceeds >5 threshold)

test_Boundary_NonceJump_AtLimit().notice: Nonce jump of exactly 5 should NOT trigger (threshold is >5)

test_DetectsBalanceDrain().notice: Test that balance drain is also detected (secondary signal)

test_DetectsImplementationSwap().notice: Core test: shouldRespond fires on implementation swap

test_DetectsNonceJump().notice: Detect nonce jump indicating rapid transaction execution

test_DetectsOwnerSetChange().notice: Detect owner set change (different signers, same count)

test_DetectsSubtleMasterCopySwap().notice: Detect subtle masterCopy swap where new implementation still has Safe functions

test_EdgeCase_EmptyDataArray().notice: Empty data array should return (false, "") -- not revert

test_EdgeCase_MalformedBytes().notice: Malformed bytes (too short to be a valid Snapshot) should return (false, "")

test_EdgeCase_SingleSample().notice: Single sample (no comparison possible) should return (false, "")

test_EdgeCase_ZeroLengthCurrentSample().notice: Zero-length bytes in data[0] should return (false, "") -- not revert

test_EdgeCase_ZeroLengthPreviousSample().notice: Zero-length bytes in data[1] should return (false, "") -- not revert

test_EndToEnd_DetectRespondContain().notice: End-to-end: Detect -> Respond -> Contain cycle using real exploit data.   This test demonstrates the FULL Drosera response path:   1. Trap collects data at the real exploit blocks   2. shouldRespond() fires with the correct threat type and payload   3. The payload is passed directly to the responder's handleIncident()   4. Responder pauses operations and logs the incident   5. While paused, further operations are blocked   6. Admin resolves the incident and unpauses   This proves payload alignment end-to-end:   trap shouldRespond() -> abi.encode(uint8, bytes) -> handleIncident(uint8, bytes)

test_ExploitReproduction_BybitAttack().notice: EXPLOIT REPRODUCTION: Replay the Bybit $1.46B hack step-by-step.   Step 1 (Block 21,895,237): Pre-exploit -- Safe proxy points to legitimate           singleton, all functions work, wallet holds ~$1.46B in ETH + LSTs.   Step 2 (Block 21,895,238): masterCopy swap -- Attacker's delegatecall           overwrites slot 0 with malicious implementation address.           Safe functions now revert (malicious contract doesn't implement them).           But ALL FUNDS ARE STILL IN THE WALLET -- 18-block response window.   Step 3 (Block 21,895,256): Drain -- Attacker calls backdoor functions on           the malicious implementation to sweep all funds.           401,346 ETH + 90,375 stETH + 8,000 mETH + 15,000 cmETH = $1.46B gone.   THIS IS WHERE DROSERA INTERVENES: Between Step 2 and Step 3.   The trap detects the masterCopy swap at Step 2 and triggers the response   contract to pause operations -- 18 blocks BEFORE the drain.

test_FullOperatorFlow().notice: Full Drosera operator simulation with 5 block samples

test_NoFalsePositive().notice: No false positive during normal operation

test_PostSwapState().notice: Post-swap: Safe functions revert, ETH still present (18-block window)

test_PreExploitState().notice: Pre-exploit: Safe functions work, balances are large

test_ResponderIntegration().notice: Verify the responder correctly receives and stores incident data

test_SampleOrdering_IdenticalSnapshots().notice: Verify that identical consecutive snapshots do not trigger

test_SampleOrdering_SwappedOrderNoFalseP.notice: Verify data[0] = newest, data[1] = previous convention is respected.         Swapping order (putting pre-exploit in data[0]) should NOT trigger,         because a "recovering" balance is not a drain.
